data.reasoningText: The user wants to run the tests in this project with `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` with crash dump collection. Since this project uses the Microsoft Testing Platform (`TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` is true), I can use the `--crashdump` option which is built into the testing platform.

For Microsoft Testing Platform (which this project uses via `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`), crash dumps are collected via the `--crashdump` flag directly.

Let me run the tests with crash dump collection enabled.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d0139e63e98b44fba01d170e5cc41aa5 && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.content: The `--crashdump` flag requires the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` NuGet package. Let me add it and retry.
data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag is not recognized directly by `dotnet test` in this way. Since this project has `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` set to true, the crash dump options need to be passed differently.

For the Microsoft Testing Platform, crash dump collection is provided by the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` package. Let me add that package and then use the `--crashdump` option.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-d0139e63e98b44fba01d170e5cc41aa5 && dotnet add package Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump --version 1.6.2 2>&1", "description": "Add CrashDump NuGet package", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content: The flag needs to be passed through to the test runner. Let me use `--` to separate it:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crashdump via -- separator

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` switch needs to be passed to the test runner, not to MSBuild. With the Microsoft Testing Platform and `dotnet test`, I need to use `--` to separate MSBuild arguments from test runner arguments. Let me try:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Actually, let me check. With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, the way to pass testing platform options through `dotnet test` is with `--`. Let me try that.

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

The `--` separates `dotnet test` options from Microsoft Testing Platform options. The `--crashdump` flag (provided by the `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump` package I added) will automatically collect a crash dump if the test host process crashes. The dump file is written to the test results directory.
